text: Make the repository hygienic for publishing
description: Clean up generated artifacts, ignore rules, placeholder content, checked-in local junk, and release-facing metadata so the repository is ready for public publishing and normal contributor workflows.

reflections.context: Prepare the repository surface for public open source release by cleaning up metadata, docs, tests, and accidental artifacts.
reflections.outcome: Completed all repository hygiene sub-aims: audited package manifests and added LICENSE, created release checklist, fixed backend test coverage and parallelism, fenced off experimental directories, and improved documentation across core packages.
reflections.effectiveness: Breaking the hygiene work into focused sub-aims (metadata, tests, experiments, checklist) allowed parallel progress and clear completion criteria. The release checklist provided a concrete definition of done.
reflections.lesson: Repository hygiene is distinct from runtime behavior. Hygiene (docs, metadata, tests, structure) can be completed independently from startup robustness (port fallback, clean-clone verification). Separating these concerns prevents scope creep.
reflections.pattern: This matches previous release-prep experience where documentation and structural cleanup provides high confidence gains early, while runtime verification requires separate focused work.

status.comment: Repository hygiene complete: manifests audited, LICENSE added, experiments fenced, tests working, docs in place. Remaining release-prep work is in runtime/startup behavior aims.
